orchestrator_notes: RT-2. Agent wasted ~21 tool calls monitoring independent background agents. Polling loop protocol is too prescriptive â€” makes monitoring mandatory even when unnecessary. Likely just a protocol text fix. Research: find the polling instructions, determine if they're overly rigid, propose targeted text change.

polling_locations_found.text: - If agents still working: resume monitoring loop (~20s intervals)
polling_locations_found.severity: PRIMARY - root cause of RT-2 waste

polling_locations_found.text: 3. Monitor agent progress via receive_messages() every 2-3 minutes
polling_locations_found.severity: SECONDARY - reasonable interval but lacks situational awareness

polling_locations_found.text: poll status via get_workflow_status() - no interval or situational guidance

current_protocol_analysis.is_polling_always_required: YES in CLI mode (continuation prompt mandates ~20s loop). NO in multi-terminal mode.
current_protocol_analysis.polling_interval: Two conflicting values: continuation prompt says ~20s, CH5 says 2-3 minutes. 20s is the problematic one.
current_protocol_analysis.differentiates_running_vs_complete: PARTIALLY. Has decision tree but once in still-working branch, polls all agents at same aggressive rate.
current_protocol_analysis.differentiates_independent_vs_dependent: NO. This is the core of the RT-2 problem.

waste_analysis.calls_per_poll_cycle: 2 MCP calls minimum (get_workflow_status + receive_messages). Up to 3 with report_progress.
waste_analysis.5_agents_10_min_at_20s: 30 cycles x 2-3 calls = 60-90 MCP tool calls. RT-2 21-call waste matches ~7 cycle window.
waste_analysis.5_agents_10_min_at_2_3_min: 3-5 cycles x 2-3 calls = 6-15 MCP tool calls. Better but still wasteful for independent agents.
waste_analysis.waste_source: BOTH frequency (20s too aggressive) and scope (polling ALL agents regardless of coordination need).

proposed_fix.recommendation: PROTOCOL TEXT FIX ONLY - no backend changes needed

changes.current: - If agents still working: resume monitoring loop (~20s intervals)
changes.proposed: - If agents still working AND need coordination (handoffs, blockers): check get_workflow_status() every 2-3 minutes. If all running agents are independent, skip continuous polling - check once before closeout.

changes.current_summary: Monitor every 2-3 min for all patterns indiscriminately
changes.proposed_summary: Situational: sequential = poll current agent only; parallel dependent = poll 2-3 min; parallel independent = do NOT poll, check once before closeout.

changes.current_summary: poll status with no interval or situational guidance
changes.proposed_summary: Background no-handoff agents: do NOT poll. Dependent agents: poll 2-3 min. Check get_workflow_status() once before closeout.

proposed_fix.adaptive_interval_rejected: Adds complexity without addressing root cause. Real fix is making monitoring optional for independent agents.

sessions.notes_for_next: Implementation session 0804b should make 3 targeted text changes: (1) build_continuation_prompt line 148 - replace ~20s interval with situational guidance, (2) _build_ch5_reference lines 838-851 - add coordination-aware polling patterns, (3) _build_claude_code_execution_prompt monitoring_section - add situational polling guidance for background agents. All changes are string literal modifications. No function signatures, backend logic, or database changes needed.
sessions.summary: Root cause confirmed: the continuation prompt (line 148) mandates a ~20s polling loop for ALL working agents. CH5 reference uses a more reasonable 2-3 min interval but still applies indiscriminately. Multi-terminal mode is already correct (says Do NOT poll). Fix is 3 protocol text changes to make monitoring situational based on coordination need. No backend changes required.
